Container.Name: Ferrite
Container.Overview: Ferrite is a Rust DNS sinkhole and filtering API server. It blocks ads and trackers at the DNS level, supports encrypted upstream resolvers, stores query statistics, and provides a web/API management surface.
Container.Requires: Ports 53 TCP/UDP and 80 TCP must be available on the selected Unraid network/IP. A custom Docker network or fixed IP is recommended because the Unraid web UI often already uses host port 80. All Ferrite config, SQLite data, blocklist cache, snapshots, and web assets are stored under /mnt/user/appdata/ferrite.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Stores Ferrite config, SQLite database, blocklist cache, snapshots, and web assets.

Config.Name: DNS TCP
Config.Description: DNS TCP listener for name resolution.

Config.Name: DNS UDP
Config.Description: DNS UDP listener for name resolution.

Config.Name: Server Release Version
Config.Description: Use latest to install the newest Ferrite release on container start. Set a fixed version, for example 0.1.1, to disable automatic server release updates on restart.

Config.Name: Web Release Version
Config.Description: Optional web UI release version. Leave blank to follow Server Release Version; set a fixed version to pin the web UI separately.

Config.Name: Panel IP
Config.Description: Optional LAN IP for the built-in fe.te A record. In bridge networking set this to the Unraid host IP, for example 192.168.1.5. Leave blank to auto-detect.

Config.Name: Panel URL
Config.Description: Optional startup-log URL for the panel shortcut. If Web UI is mapped to a non-80 host port, set this to http://fe.te:<port>.

Config.Name: GitHub Release Token
Config.Description: Optional GitHub token used when the container downloads server and web releases on start. Leave blank for public releases. Set a token only if you hit GitHub API rate limits (anonymous requests are capped at 60/hour per IP) or pull from a private mirror.
